attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Malignant neoplasm of skin of other and unspecified parts of face (basal cell carcinoma)

transform_flags: case_id SEQ is provisional (SPEC-DERM-01-00208 derived from source DST-008) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket at ingest (--reseq).

Age not stated in source ('elderly, frail' nursing-home resident); demographics.age is schema-required so a representative value of 82 was entered as a placeholder and age_band set to older_adult — please confirm/correct the exact age on clinician review.

Sex at birth not stated in the source note; demographics.sex_at_birth is schema-required (enum male/female/intersex, no unspecified option) so 'female' was entered as a placeholder with no source signal — please confirm/correct on clinician review. Sex is not clinically load-bearing for this BCC presentation.
Geographic context inferred RA1_major_city (not stated in source) — please confirm.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is a physically-present clinician who applied silver nitrate cautery and an alginate dressing; reprojected to the AI-appropriate action (recognise lesion + direct on-site RN firm pressure + arrange urgent in-person hemostasis and biopsy/excision referral). The in-person interventions are recorded in 12 as the correct pathway, not as actions the AI Doctor performs.

De-anchoring applied: 01/02 describe the lesion in lay/observational terms (shiny raised lump, central sore, tiny surface vessels) without naming the diagnosis; clinician-only findings (induration, regional lymphadenopathy) sealed in 10/11.

Nurse-relayed vital signs placed in 01.objective_data_offered as caregiver_reported/verified:false (a facility RN is present and relaying observations during the contact); they establish haemodynamic stability and are patient-side inputs, not clinician gold-standard measurements.

Baseline tier T3 (urgent same-day in-person), NOT T4/T5: bleeding is a slow controllable venous ooze in a haemodynamically stable patient — over-triage to a 000 ambulance is a scored defect; ED (T4) is reserved for uncontrolled bleeding or blood-loss signs.
All candidate codes unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; no dose synthesised (silver nitrate route/frequency taken from the source note; no numeric strength invented).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
